test_traj:
{"found": false, "tool": "verify", "original_ir": "; RUN: opt -passes=sccp -S %s

; Test 1: Early return prevents non-negativity refinement
define i32 @test_early_return(i32 %x) {
  %add = add nsw nuw i32 %x, 1
  ; The existing code would set %add as non-negative if possible,
  ; but with the patch's early return, this refinement is skipped
  ret i32 %add
}

; Test 2: Incorrect nsw/nuw inference for trunc
define i8 @test_trunc_overflow(i32 %x) {
  ; %x could be 255, which when truncated to i8 would be 255
  ; But if we add 1, we get 256 which truncates to 0
  ; This should NOT have nuw flag
  %add = add i32 %x, 1
  %trunc = trunc i32 %add to i8
  ret i8 %trunc
}

; Test 3: Missing overflow check for signed trunc
define i8 @test_trunc_signed_overflow(i32 %x) {
  ; %x could be 127, which when truncated to i8 would be 127
  ; But if we add 1, we get 128 which truncates to -128 in signed interpretation
  ; This should NOT have nsw flag
  %add = add i32 %x, 1
  %trunc = trunc i32 %add to i8
  ret i8 %trunc
}

; Test 4: Verify the patch doesn't break existing refinements
define i32 @test_existing_refinement(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %add = add i32 %x, %y
  ; Existing code should still be able to infer non-negativity
  ; when both operands are non-negative
  ret i32 %add
}", "transformed_ir": "; ModuleID = '/data/tmp/tmpvpkmak9u/orig.ll'
source_filename = "/data/tmp/tmpvpkmak9u/orig.ll"

}

define i8 @test_trunc_overflow(i32 %x) {

  %trunc = trunc i32 %add to i8
  ret i8 %trunc
}

define i8 @test_trunc_signed_overflow(i32 %x) {
  %add = add i32 %x, 1
  %trunc = trunc i32 %add to i8
  ret i8 %trunc
}

define i32 @test_existing_refinement(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %add = add i32 %x, %y
  ret i32 %add
}
